message.content:
Create UI component files for a Stan Store clone at C:\Users\User\stan-io. Use CSS variables (var(--bg-surface), var(--border-default), var(--text-primary), etc.) for theming support. Use lucide-react for icons. Use the Write tool to create each file.

Create these directories first with mkdir if needed.

### File 1: `C:\Users\User\stan-io\components\ui\Button.tsx`
Button with variants: primary (bg-[var(--accent)] text-white), secondary (border), ghost, danger. Sizes: sm, md, lg. Support icon, loading state.

### File 2: `C:\Users\User\stan-io\components\ui\Card.tsx`
Card with bg-[var(--bg-surface)] border-[var(--border-default)] rounded-xl. Props: hover, padding (none/sm/md/lg).

### File 3: `C:\Users\User\stan-io\components\ui\Badge.tsx`
Badge with variants: default, success, warning, error, accent. Small text, rounded.

### File 4: `C:\Users\User\stan-io\components\ui\ThemeToggle.tsx`
Sun/Moon toggle using useTheme from ../../lib/theme-context. Import { Sun, Moon } from lucide-react.

### File 5: `C:\Users\User\stan-io\components\ui\Modal.tsx`
Reusable modal component with backdrop, centered content, title, close button (X icon). Props: isOpen, onClose, title, children, size ('sm'|'md'|'lg').

### File 6: `C:\Users\User\stan-io\components\ui\Tabs.tsx`
Horizontal tab component. Props: tabs (array of {id, label}), activeTab, onChange. Styled with accent underline for active tab.

### File 7: `C:\Users\User\stan-io\components\ui\EmptyState.tsx`
Empty state placeholder with icon, title, description, and optional action button. Props: icon (React.ElementType), title, description, actionLabel?, onAction?.

### File 8: `C:\Users\User\stan-io\components\ui\StatsCard.tsx`
Stats card for dashboard showing label, value (big number), change percentage (green up or red down), and optional icon. Props: label, value, change?, changeLabel?, icon?.

### File 9: `C:\Users\User\stan-io\components\ui\DataTable.tsx`
Simple data table component. Props: columns (array of {key, label, render?}), data (array of objects), onRowClick?. Styled with border-[var(--border-default)], striped rows using var(--bg-elevated).

### File 10: `C:\Users\User\stan-io\components\ui\Toggle.tsx`
Simple toggle switch component. Props: enabled, onChange, label?. Purple/accent when on, gray when off.

All components should use CSS variables for colors and support both light and dark themes automatically. Keep the code clean and minimal.

content.text: I'll create all the UI component files for the Stan Store clone. Let me start by creating the directory structure.
Now I'll create all 10 component files in parallel.

All 10 UI component files have been created. Here is a summary of each file:

---

### File 1: `C:\Users\User\stan-io\components\ui\Button.tsx`
A versatile button component with four variants (`primary`, `secondary`, `ghost`, `danger`) and three sizes (`sm`, `md`, `lg`). Supports an `icon` prop with configurable position (`left`/`right`), a `loading` state that shows a spinning `Loader2` icon from lucide-react, and extends all native button HTML attributes. The primary variant uses `bg-[var(--accent)]` with white text.

### File 2: `C:\Users\User\stan-io\components\ui\Card.tsx`
A card container with `bg-[var(--bg-surface)]`, `border-[var(--border-default)]`, and `rounded-xl`. Supports four padding levels (`none`, `sm`, `md`, `lg`), an optional `hover` prop that adds shadow on hover, and an `onClick` handler that adds cursor-pointer styling and keyboard accessibility (`Enter`/`Space`).

### File 3: `C:\Users\User\stan-io\components\ui\Badge.tsx`
An inline badge component with five variants: `default` (uses `--bg-elevated`), `success` (emerald), `warning` (amber), `error` (red), and `accent` (uses `--accent` variable). Each variant has subtle background tinting and matching border/text colors. Rendered as a small `rounded-full` span.

### File 4: `C:\Users\User\stan-io\components\ui\ThemeToggle.tsx`
A Sun/Moon toggle button importing `useTheme` from `../../lib/theme-context`. Displays a `Moon` icon in light mode and a `Sun` icon in dark mode. Note: this file was modified externally after initial creation -- the current version uses a default export and slightly different styling.

### File 5: `C:\Users\User\stan-io\components\ui\Modal.tsx`
A reusable modal with a semi-transparent `backdrop-blur-sm` overlay. Supports three sizes (`sm`, `md`, `lg`), an optional `title` with a close button in the header, and `Escape` key dismissal. When open, it locks body scroll. Uses the `X` icon from lucide-react for the close button.

### File 6: `C:\Users\User\stan-io\components\ui\Tabs.tsx`
A horizontal tab bar component accepting `tabs` (array of `{id, label}`), `activeTab`, and `onChange`. The active tab gets `text-[var(--accent)]` coloring with a bottom accent-colored underline bar. Uses proper `role="tablist"` and `role="tab"` ARIA attributes.

### File 7: `C:\Users\User\stan-io\components\ui\EmptyState.tsx`
An empty state placeholder centered vertically and horizontally. Accepts an `icon` (as `React.ElementType`), `title`, `description`, and optional `actionLabel`/`onAction` for a primary CTA button. The icon renders inside a rounded `--bg-elevated` container.

### File 8: `C:\Users\User\stan-io\components\ui\StatsCard.tsx`
A dashboard stats card built on top of the `Card` component. Displays a `label`, a large `value`, an optional `change` percentage (with `TrendingUp`/`TrendingDown` icons in green/red), a `changeLabel`, and an optional icon in a rounded elevated container.

### File 9: `C:\Users\User\stan-io\components\ui\DataTable.tsx`
A generic data table using TypeScript generics (`DataTable<T>`). Columns are defined with `{key, label, render?}` where `render` allows custom cell rendering. Rows alternate between `--bg-surface` and `--bg-elevated` for striped styling. Supports `onRowClick` and shows a "No data available" message when the data array is empty.

### File 10: `C:\Users\User\stan-io\components\ui\Toggle.tsx`
A toggle switch component with `enabled` and `onChange` props. When enabled, the track uses `bg-[var(--accent)]`; when off, it uses gray (with dark mode variant). The thumb is white with a smooth sliding transition. Supports an optional `label` and uses `role="switch"` with `aria-checked` for accessibility.
